More Than Dog Training
Client Onboarding

Welcome to More Than Dog Training

Thank you for choosing More Than Dog Training. Our mission is to help you build a better relationship with your dog by helping you truly understand how to communicate with them. We’ll teach you — the owner — to train with a genuine knowledge of how your dog sees the world, giving you the tools to address any behavior that may arise long after our work together has ended.

Whether you’re feeling frustrated and ready for a change, or simply getting ahead of things with a new puppy or family addition, your goals are absolutely within reach — they just take patience and consistent effort from both of you. You are no longer on this journey alone. Your dog is lucky to have an owner dedicated enough to seek out proper training, and I look forward to every step of this process with you.

Before We Begin

A Few Things to Know

Leash & Containment

Please have your dog safely contained using a standard 4–6 foot leash, crate, or fenced yard for all sessions. Retractable leashes are not permitted during our time together.

Collar & Harness Fit

Before each session, check that your dog’s collar or harness is snug enough that you can fit only two fingers between it and your dog’s neck or body. This is important for the safety of everyone involved.

Skip the Pre-Session Meal

Please avoid feeding your dog any meals or treats 2–3 hours before our session. Training with food rewards works best when your dog has an appetite — it leads to better focus and faster results.

Response Policy

Communication Hours

I want you to feel supported throughout this process — and part of that means being upfront about when you can expect to hear back from me. Whether you call, text, or email, all messages are returned within these hours only.

Monday – Friday
9:00 am – 6:00 pm
Saturday
9:00 am – 12:00 pm
Sunday
Unavailable

Messages received after hours — including evenings and all day Sunday — will be returned the next time I’m available. A Saturday night message, for example, won’t receive a reply until Monday morning. This is not me ignoring you — it’s simply how I protect my time so I can show up fully for every client.

Next Steps

Your Onboarding Checklist

1

Complete Your Registration Form

Tell us a little about you and your dog so we can hit the ground running. This only takes a few minutes and helps me tailor our sessions to your specific goals.

Register Now
2

Review & Sign Your Client Agreement

Please read through the client agreement carefully and sign before your first session. This covers everything we need in place to get started together.

View & Sign
3

Submit Your Payment

Once your registration and agreement are complete, I’ll reach out personally with your payment details. Most clients pay via Venmo — Stripe is also available with a 3% processing fee.

Venmo
4

Follow Along on Social Media

Stay connected between sessions for training tips, updates, and a behind-the-scenes look at what we do.

Instagram TikTok
Additional Resources

Client Training Guide

This guide is yours to keep and refer back to throughout our time together. It’s not required reading before we begin — think of it as a reference you can return to anytime a question comes up between sessions.
